# Slice 2.6 — code review ledger (rebuilt)

Spec: [`spec.md`](./spec.md) · Design: [`design.md`](./design.md) · Plan: [`plan.md`](./plan.md).

The prior 24-commit attempt (merge unique into index) was discarded — it merged two distinct schema elements into one node, forcing back the tree-massaging the slice exists to remove. Branch reset to `origin/main`; rebuilding the small correct slice per the design.

## Findings log

### Rebuild round 1 (reviewer) — no findings

Reviewed commits `061ab1538..c57c0c191` (4 commits, 18 files, net −164). No must-fix / should-fix / low findings. Verdict: **SATISFIED**.

### W7 round 1 (reviewer) — no findings; design-call verdict: implementer's shape is correct

Reviewed commit `7fad7178f` (11 files) — replaces the rejected `flattenReferencedNamespace` flag with namespace-NODE behavior. No must-fix / should-fix / low findings. Verdict: **SATISFIED**.

## Round notes

### Rebuild round 1 (reviewer) — SATISFIED

Reviewed the fresh deletion-dominated slice against `design.md` (the authority). The corrected model — one node per schema element, the differ compares two derived trees and does nothing else — is faithfully implemented. No findings.

**1 — differ runs on trees as derived.** `diff-tree-normalization.ts` deleted (211 lines); `normalizePostgresActualForDiff` deleted from the Postgres file. Both `diff-database-schema.ts` now call `diffSchemas(expected, actual)` (Postgres `paddedActual`) with nothing between derivation and diff — Postgres retains only its pre-existing owned-namespace `not-expected` filter (unchanged). Grep-clean confirmed: zero matches for `resolveSemanticSatisfaction`, `normalizeFlatActualForDiff`, `normalizePostgresActualForDiff`, `diff-tree-normalization`, `fk-schema-normalization`, `neutralizeFlatExpectedFkSchemas`, `isUniqueConstraintSatisfied`, `isIndexSatisfied`, `SemanticSatisfaction*`, `isSuperfluousConstraintOnlyNotEqual`, `isBareUniqueIndexExtra`, `divergesOnlyBySuperfluousConstraint`, and no `constraint` marker on `SqlIndexIR`.

**2 — two clean structural nodes.** `SqlUniqueIR` is untouched apart from removing its stale "ACCEPTED-UNDER-PROTEST DEBT — DELETE IN SLICE 2.6" docblock (the design keeps the node, so the note was wrong); `isEqualTo` is still `this.id === node.id` (id-identity). `SqlIndexIR.isEqualTo` is now `this.unique === node.unique && this.type === node.type && indexOptionsLooselyEqual(this.options, node.options)` — genuinely symmetric, the `(!this.unique || node.unique)` satisfaction rule removed. The two real normalizations stay: `indexOptionsLooselyEqual` (reloptions string-coercion) and the introspection-side btree→undefined `type` normalization (`control-adapter.ts:1078-1081`, untouched), so a default-method index does not false-drift. The deleted `isUniqueConstraintSatisfied`/`isIndexSatisfied` were verified caller-less on the merge-base — the only reference outside their definition/barrel was a doc comment in the (now-deleted) `diff-tree-normalization.ts`.

**3 — behaviour flips correct and non-tautological.** `schema-verify.verdict.test.ts` pins each with exact reason+nodekind: (a) contract `@@unique` vs live unique **index** → `['sql-unique','not-found']` (fails both) + `['sql-index','not-expected']` (strict-only), both modes fail; (b) contract `@@index` vs live unique **constraint** → `['sql-index','not-found']` + `['sql-unique','not-expected']`, both modes fail (was lenient-pass); (c) stray live unique index → `['sql-index','not-expected']`, strict-fails/lenient-passes (was invisible); (d) the round-trip contract `@@unique` vs live unique **constraint** → `SqlUniqueIR` pairs `SqlUniqueIR` by id-identity → clean both modes (the must-not-regress case). The new consequence of symmetric equality — contract `@@index` vs a live **unique index** (both `SqlIndexIR`, differ on `unique`) → not-equal → `indexIncompatible`/recreate — is correct: a plain index and a unique index on the same columns are genuinely different objects (Postgres cannot alter uniqueness in place). `planner.unique-index-structural.test.ts` pins the planner side end-to-end: flip (a) → `[unique.user.user_email_key: additive]` with the destructive index drop suppressed by the existing additive control-policy disposition; the `@@index`-vs-unique-index conflict → `[{kind:'indexIncompatible'}]`; flip (b) → `[index.user.user_email_idx: additive]` with the constraint drop suppressed; and name-only differences → zero ops.

**4 — FK-fold is byte-neutral and target-agnostic.** `convertForeignKey(fk, flattenReferencedNamespace)` sets `referencedSchema: fk.target.namespaceId` unconditionally and adds `resolvedReferencedNamespace: ''` only when flattening — exactly what the deleted `neutralizeFlatExpectedFkSchemas` produced (it rewrote `resolvedReferencedNamespace` to `''` and preserved every other FK field). The option lives on `ContractToSchemaIROptions`, defaults `false`, is threaded through both derivation entry points, and describes single-schema-ness, not a target — no `if (sqlite)` in shared code. SQLite passes it via `sqliteContractToSchema`; Postgres omits it and re-resolves FK schemas downstream (its constructor default is never observed at the diff). The new `contractToSchemaIR — flattenReferencedNamespace` derivation test pins both states (`UNBOUND_NAMESPACE_ID` default vs `''`); the FK verdict tests changed only harness plumbing (the `runVerdict` helper swapped the two deleted calls for `flattenReferencedNamespace: true`), no FK assertion moved.

**5 — the fence held.** `git diff origin/main -- schema-verify.ts` is byte-EMPTY. Exactly 8 production files changed (the two nodes, the deleted reconciliation file, `sql-schema-diff.ts` predicate deletion, derivation, the diff barrel, both `diff-database-schema.ts`) — nothing in the general strict/lenient extra-tolerance (`isStrictOnlyExtra`, base strict gate), control-policy `dispositionForCategory`, cross-space ownership, or either planner (`issue-planner.ts`/`planner.ts` untouched — they already carry independent `SqlUniqueIR` and `SqlIndexIR` arms, so a unique-vs-index mismatch is naturally missing+extra). Flip (c) is delivered purely by the untouched axis-2 grading.

**6 — the fixture fix is honest.** `sqlite/runner.across-spaces.test.ts` declares a contract with BOTH `@@unique` and `@@index` on `email` (two distinct declared elements). The hand-written live DDL created only `email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE` (the constraint); the declared index was masked before only by the satisfaction fuzz (`isIndexSatisfied`: a unique constraint satisfied an index requirement). Adding `CREATE INDEX user_email_idx ON user (email)` makes the live schema honestly carry both elements the contract declares — a correct alignment, not a workaround hiding a regression (the default index name `user_email_idx` matches the contract index node's derived name, so it pairs cleanly). No other fixture moved; `fixtures:check` clean.

### W7 round 1 (reviewer) — SATISFIED, and the Postgres compensation is the RIGHT shape

Reviewed commit `7fad7178f` (the FK half): the rejected `flattenReferencedNamespace` flag is replaced by namespace-node behavior. Purely FK — no unique/index file touched (`sql-index-ir`/`sql-unique-ir`/`sql-schema-diff`/reconciliation all absent from the W7 file list), so the four-commit slice I approved is unaffected. No findings.

**Mechanism.** `NamespaceBase.isUnbound` is a concrete getter (`this.id === UNBOUND_NAMESPACE_ID`) — the single encapsulated place the sentinel comparison lives. A base impl (not a singleton override) is genuinely required: non-singleton namespaces that occupy the unbound slot (`SqliteDatabase` with tables) must also answer true, which a singleton-only override would miss. The `Namespace`/`SqlNamespace` interfaces carry `isUnbound?: boolean` (optional, mirroring the `qualifyTable?` JSON-satisfier precedent). `convertForeignKey(fk, storage)` resolves `storage.namespaces[fk.target.namespaceId]?.isUnbound`: unbound → stamps no `referencedSchema` (the FK id renders absence as `''`); bound → stamps verbatim; absent-from-storage (cross-space) → `?.isUnbound` is `undefined`, so it keeps the coordinate. The derivation test pins all three (unbound id `foreign-key:authorId->.User(id)`, bound `->accounting.User`, cross-space `->other_contract_ns.User`), plus a direct `isUnbound` unit test.

**#1 byte-identity — exact.** The Postgres re-map changed `...ifDefined('referencedSchema', fk.referencedSchema)` → `referencedSchema: fk.referencedSchema ?? UNBOUND_NAMESPACE_ID`, and feeds the same `?? UNBOUND_NAMESPACE_ID` into `resolveDdlSchemaForNamespaceStorage`. For an unbound target, main stamped `fk.referencedSchema = UNBOUND_NAMESPACE_ID` (present) and W7 stamps it absent, so `absent ?? UNBOUND_NAMESPACE_ID` restores exactly the pre-slice value; for bound/cross-space `fk.referencedSchema` is present so the `??` is inert. Both `referencedSchema` and the re-resolved `resolvedReferencedNamespace` come out identical to main — corroborated by `fixtures:check` clean + pg target 744/744 + adapter/pgvector/supabase green.

**#2 design call — implementer's approach is the right shape, NOT a smell.** The alternative (stamp `resolvedReferencedNamespace: ''` for unbound while leaving `referencedSchema = namespaceId`, so Postgres is untouched) does avoid the compensation, and I traced it as byte-correct at the diff layer — but it is WORSE on the design's own terms. `fk.target.namespaceId` for an unbound target *is* `UNBOUND_NAMESPACE_ID` (`'__unbound__'`), so keeping `referencedSchema = namespaceId` leaves the `__unbound__` sentinel sitting in the SQLite **schema-IR** FK node — exactly what design §Derivation forbids ("no `__unbound__` string ever entering schema-IR"). That is precisely what `main` did (its neutralized SQLite FK carried `referencedSchema = __unbound__`), and the whole point of W7 is to remove it. The implementer's "omit `referencedSchema` when unbound" makes the shared schema-IR sentinel-free (grep-clean `__unbound__` in schema-ir/src = 0) and additionally makes the expected FK node structurally match the introspected one (both absent) instead of merely pairing by id. The cost — Postgres re-stamping `?? UNBOUND_NAMESPACE_ID` — is correctly localized to Postgres's OWN target layer, where a raw coordinate is genuinely needed to qualify the REFERENCES clause and to feed DDL-schema resolution; and because Postgres **re-resolves** `resolvedReferencedNamespace` from `referencedSchema` (it never reads the family's value), the family-side absence never leaks into Postgres pairing. The two goals "sentinel-free shared layer" and "zero Postgres edits" are in genuine tension, and prioritizing the clean shared layer is the correct call. The `?? UNBOUND_NAMESPACE_ID` literal is a minor sentinel-as-value wart in the Postgres target; byte-correct and tracked by the filed follow-up (encapsulate the const) — acceptable-for-now, does not need to be cleaner in this slice.

**Also verified.** FK verdict tests (`schema-verify.verdict.test.ts` §foreign-keys) unchanged — the only edit is the harness dropping `flattenReferencedNamespace: true`, no FK assertion moved. The two `sql-foreign-key-ir.test.ts` data neutralizations (`'__unbound__'` → `'app_ns'`) never tested unbound semantics: `SqlForeignKeyIR` has no unbound behavior, it just uses `referencedSchema` as an id string, so the sentinel was arbitrary test data; renaming to a bound namespace keeps the sentinel out of schema-ir tests too and the id/pairing assertions still hold. Grep-clean: `flattenReferencedNamespace` = 0; `__unbound__`/`UNBOUND_NAMESPACE_ID` in `schema-ir/src` = 0. Fence still holds (`schema-verify.ts` untouched by W7). Full gate green.

## Orchestrator notes

- 2026-07-10: operator rejected the merge-into-one-node model as a violation of "one node per element, no tree massaging." Branch reset to origin/main; corrected design in `design.md`. The correct slice: delete the reconciliation, keep the two nodes, fold FK resolution into derivation, `SqlIndexIR.isEqualTo` symmetric. Deliberate behaviour change (satisfaction cases become drift), fenced to unique/index (axis-2 extra-tolerance untouched).
